description: Machine-readable map of the Redis runtime-mirror namespaces for the healthcare AI concierge. Redis is the fast runtime mirror; Postgres/SQLite is authoritative. jsonShape values are the REAL top-level value shapes captured from the live Redis (127.0.0.1:6381); 'object'/'array[n] of {...}' annotations are summaries of nested structure, not invented fields.

backendSelection.requireRedisEnv: BRAINSTY_REQUIRE_REDIS=1 (or runtime env production/prod/staging/production-candidate) makes Redis mandatory; initializeRuntimeCache fails loud otherwise
backendSelection.metricsEndpoint: /api/health -> redisRuntime.cacheMetrics (getRuntimeCacheMetrics: hits, misses, sets, deletes, errors, total, hitRate, lastBackend)

brainsty:capability-catalog.purpose: Postgres-sourced planner-facing capability/process catalog mirror (metadata-only promptTable + pointer entries; the HOW is hydrated separately via hydrateCapabilityPointer). Evicted on capability quarantine/demote/lifecycle-sync.

jsonShape.promptTable: array[19] of {portfolioId, kind, title, whenToUse, whyUse, shortDescription, approvalScope, pointer, score}
jsonShape.entries: object map of portfolioId -> {portfolioId, kind, pointer} (e.g. process:portal_readonly_lookup, workflow:claim_status_navigation, skill:insurance_portal_browser, tool:openclaw_authenticated_browser, graph_path:approval_interrupt_resume)

brainsty:capability-portfolio.purpose: Per-turn capability portfolio fullPayload built from the context packet's workflowArchitecture (workflows, skills, tools, graph paths). Read back to dereference LLM-planner-selected pointers into full hydrated entries.

jsonShape.entries: object map of portfolioId -> entry object (workflow:*, skill:*, tool:*, graph:* keys; each entry carries kind/title/pointer/score and a nested hydrate payload)

brainsty:llm-output-index.purpose: Bounded (max 20) index of LLM outputs per session: output hash + pointer + parsed summary per step. rawOutputStored is always false (only hashes/summaries kept, not raw PHI-bearing output).

jsonShape.entries: array[1] of {outputId, pointer, step, graphTraceId, model, modelTier, mode, outputHash, rawOutputStored, parsedSummary, createdAt}

brainsty:runtime-context.purpose: Cross-turn runtime context manifest: compacted achieved checkpoints (merged with prior cached checkpoints for resume), latest checkpoint, prior decision pointers, prompt compaction settings, and capability summary. Source of cross-turn hydration.

jsonShape.achievedCheckpoints: array[1] of {checkpointId, stepName, createdAt, stateVersion, workflow, routeReason, contextPacketId, sourcePointerCount, evidenceObservationStatus}
jsonShape.priorDecisionPointers: array[1] of {checkpointId, stepName, workflow, routeReason, sourcePointerCount, contextPacketId}
jsonShape.capabilitySummary: array[5] of {workflowKey, routeScore, executableNow, missingDataPointerCount, disabledToolCount}

brainsty:runtime-vector-index.purpose: Deterministic local lexical term-vector index over portfolio/checkpoint/llm-output documents, scored (cosine) against the current user input. No external embedding provider (embeddingProvider=none_local_fallback).

jsonShape.topMatches: array[2] of {docId, kind, pointer, label, sourceId, textHash, score}
jsonShape.docs: array[18] of {docId, kind, pointer, label, sourceId, textHash, score}

brainsty:worker-state.purpose: Per-session stateful OpenClaw worker runtime read back across dispatches/turns: bounded (max 10) dispatch history + latest dispatch (workflow, skill, execution mode, browser readiness). Authoritative record remains the DB (worker_continuations, source pointers, audit).

jsonShape.dispatchHistory: array[1] of {dispatchedAt, workflow, skillKey, executionMode, plannerSelectedSkillKeys, hydratedCapabilityCount, workerPlanId, browserReadinessTier, browserProductionReady, browserCdpUrl}

brainsty:idempotency.reader: (none, by design) — authoritative dedupe is the Postgres UNIQUE(idempotency_key) on workflow_checkpoint_runs
brainsty:idempotency.purpose: Losable fast-path SETNX lock for exactly-once worker dispatch. NOT load-bearing: the Redis lock is best-effort; correctness is enforced by the Postgres UNIQUE constraint. NOTE: this namespace may not be present in a live dump because the lock is short-lived (600s) and only exists during an in-flight dispatch; documented from dispatchIdempotency.mjs source.
